statement: Bundle plans and semantic clustering should reference a semantic pipeline identity (pipeline id/tag + version) in addition to a semantic level (l1/l2/l3), so "cluster on L2" means "cluster on L2 produced by pipeline P".
rationale: Today bundle plans choose a source semantic level only, which becomes scientifically ambiguous once multiple semantic view variants exist. A pipeline identity makes bundle inputs reproducible and keeps corrected bundle comparisons comparable across model/prompt changes.
keyAssumptions: Semantic clustering/bundling will be used as a first-class ablation surface in the next pilot matrix.
Multiple semantic pipelines per evidence set will exist (at least in debugging).
validationCriteria: Bundle plan materialization can deterministically select the same text surface given the same pipeline id + level.
Experiments can be rerun without silently changing bundle/clustering inputs.
critiquePoints: If pipeline variants are rare, this is more schema and UI complexity than needed.
questions: Should bundle plans store a direct view reference (evidence_view ids) or just pipeline id + level?
